evals.prompt: I need to run a quick SQL query on our Databricks cluster to see the top 10 rows from prod_catalog.sales.orders. We're on the premium profile. Can you do that for me?
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses scripts/cli.py run with --lang sql to execute the query on the cluster, shows the command used, and explains what the output means.
expectations: Uses scripts/cli.py run (not raw API calls or databricks sql execute)
Passes --lang sql
Passes --profile premium
Asks for or looks up the cluster ID before running
The SQL query targets prod_catalog.sales.orders with LIMIT 10

evals.prompt: I wrote a migration script at /tmp/migrate_customers.py that needs to run on the Databricks cluster — specifically on the general-purpose cluster (not the GPU one). It reads from a ARGS dict so I need to pass catalog='prod' and dry_run=True to it. Profile is premium.
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses scripts/cli.py run with --file, --args JSON, --cluster-id for the general-purpose cluster, and --profile premium.
expectations: Uses scripts/cli.py run with --file /tmp/migrate_customers.py
Passes --args with valid JSON containing catalog and dry_run keys
Uses the general-purpose cluster ID (not the GPU cluster <gpu-cluster-id>)
Passes --profile premium
Does NOT use the GPU cluster

evals.prompt: The notebook I uploaded to Databricks yesterday is failing. How do I check what the error was? The run ID is 194658749253431 and I think it's on the premium profile. Also how do I re-run it after fixing the error?
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses the jobs runs get-output API to fetch the error and explains the fix-and-retry cycle.
expectations: Uses databricks api get /api/2.1/jobs/runs/get-output with the task run ID
Explains the difference between the top-level run_id and the task run_id
Reads error and error_trace fields from the response
Explains the fix → re-upload → rerun cycle
Uses --profile premium

evals.prompt: I have a notebook already uploaded at /Users/me@company.com/notebooks/train_model on Databricks. I want to run it as a one-time ad-hoc job on the GPU cluster with parameters sample=500 and epochs=3. Profile is premium.
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses databricks jobs submit with the correct tasks-array JSON structure, targeting the GPU cluster.
expectations: Uses databricks jobs submit (not jobs run-now or scripts/run.py)
JSON body uses a tasks array with task_key, existing_cluster_id, and notebook_task
Does NOT put existing_cluster_id or notebook_task at the top level
Uses the GPU cluster ID <gpu-cluster-id>
Passes base_parameters with sample and epochs
Uses --profile premium

evals.prompt: I want to check what Python and Spark versions are running on the GPU cluster. Can you quickly execute something to find out?
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses scripts/cli.py run with inline Python code that prints sys.version and spark.version.
expectations: Uses scripts/cli.py run with --code (not --file)
The code imports sys and prints sys.version or sys.version_info
The code prints spark.version
Uses the GPU cluster ID <gpu-cluster-id>
Uses --lang python or lets it default

evals.prompt: I need to run a local Python script /tmp/etl_pipeline.py on the general-purpose cluster. But I don't want the execution context destroyed after it runs because I need to inspect variables. Profile is premium.
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses scripts/cli.py run with --file and --no-destroy flags.
expectations: Uses scripts/cli.py run with --file /tmp/etl_pipeline.py
Passes --no-destroy to keep the context open
Uses the general-purpose cluster (not GPU)
Passes --profile premium
Mentions that the context will remain open for further commands

evals.prompt: What tables are available in the analytics schema of the prod catalog? Profile is premium.
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses scripts/cli.py tables to list all tables in prod.analytics as a formatted table showing name, type, and description.
expectations: Uses scripts/cli.py tables (not databricks tables list directly)
Passes --catalog prod and --schema analytics
Passes --profile premium
Does NOT use scripts/cli.py run or a cluster

evals.prompt: Can you describe the table prod.analytics.customer_orders? I want to know the columns, their types, and any descriptions. Profile is premium.
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses scripts/cli.py describe to show full column-level metadata for the table.
expectations: Uses scripts/cli.py describe (not databricks tables get directly)
Passes the fully qualified name prod.analytics.customer_orders as a positional argument
Passes --profile premium
Shows columns with types, nullable flag, and descriptions

evals.prompt: I'm on Windows and trying to run the Databricks CLI script. The scripts/cli.py uses a shebang line `#!/usr/bin/env python3` and when I run it directly it fails. Also `python3` isn't a thing on Windows. How do I run it?
evals.expected_output: Claude explains: (1) shebang lines are ignored on Windows — run the script as `python scripts/cli.py` or `uv run python scripts/cli.py` instead of `./scripts/cli.py`; (2) use `python` not `python3` on Windows, or use `uv run` which is cross-platform; (3) alternatively set up a PowerShell alias. Shows the corrected invocation for all subsequent CLI commands using `uv run python scripts/cli.py run ...`.
expectations: Explains that shebang lines are ignored on Windows
Shows the correct Windows invocation: python scripts/cli.py or uv run python scripts/cli.py
Does not suggest installing WSL as the only option
Demonstrates that subsequent commands (run, etc.) work the same way with the corrected invocation
